summary: Spec approved for full cross-platform support (Windows/Mac/Linux) with three enforcement layers: CI matrix (3 OSes), 2 ESLint rules (no-unix-shell-command, no-hardcoded-path-separator), and a root-level platform parity test suite scanning 5 anti-patterns.

decisions.what: Full native support on all 3 OSes, not WSL-only
decisions.why: Users shouldn't need WSL to use harness on Windows

decisions.what: Three enforcement layers: CI + ESLint + test suite
decisions.why: Defense in depth — catch issues at authoring time, lint time, and CI time

decisions.what: Minimal CI matrix (3 OS x Node 22 only)
decisions.why: Biggest win is going from 1 OS to 3. Second Node version adds cost without current need

decisions.what: Node.js scripts using fs.rm()/fs.cp() instead of rimraf/cpy-cli
decisions.why: Node 22 has stable recursive fs APIs. Zero new dependencies
Platform safety is the same category as existing architectural constraints

decisions.what: Single root-level platform parity test suite
decisions.why: Repo-wide structural checks belong in one place, not duplicated per package

decisions.what: Parallel development on feature branch, atomic merge
decisions.why: main stays green throughout. No window for regressions between fixes and enforcement
